title: missing_evidence_followup_pack_2026_07_11 (1)
normalized_content:
Missing Evidence Follow-Up Pack for Incoming
Quality Event
Organization: Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
Event Context: Follow-up evidence requested after discrepancies in audit, complaint,
deviation, and supporting records for the new VISK sterile event involving FPP-2001
Related Primary Event Package: Incoming quality event package dated 2026-07-11
Purpose: Supplemental evidence set for system testing when Helix requests missing records,
contradictory data, raw evidence, and clarification material
This follow-up pack is designed to fill the specific missing pieces identified in the initial
event package while still preserving a realistic level of uncertainty. GMP documentation
systems are expected to maintain linked records, raw evidence, review trails, and
contemporaneous explanations for discrepancies, especially where batch disposition,
equipment status, training qualification, calibration raw data, and audit-trail completeness are
in question.[web:65][web:76][web:86]
The pack does not declare final root cause. Instead, it supplies the kinds of secondary records
a quality unit might pull when an investigation platform requests missing evidence: complete
pages, raw logs, audit-trail excerpts, photo transcriptions, shipping data, qualification proofs,
and clarifying statements. Missing records, unclear signatures, and contradictory operational
detail are common focal points in audit and investigation follow-up because documentation
must demonstrate actual control rather than the mere existence of
procedures.[web:77][web:86][web:88]
Follow-up scope
This pack addresses the following known gaps from the initial package:[web:65][web:86]
● Exact batch identity conflict between V20012607002 and V20012607003.
● Missing batch record pages and release/disposition pages.
● Missing complaint photo files and shipment context.
● Contradictory equipment-status information.
● Missing calibration raw data page.
● Incomplete supplier component details.
● Unclear operator qualification and contract-staff identity.
● Partial environmental monitoring assessment.
● Preliminary-only laboratory evidence.
● Missing dispatch/quarantine chronology.

Document 1: Batch genealogy clarification sheet
Document ID: VISK-QA-RPT-2026-0051-R0
Version: R0
Owner: Meera Iyer
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-UTL-002
Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0102-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002; V20012607003
Related Employees: EMP-0101, EMP-0202, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Purpose: Resolve conflicting references to batches 07002 and 07003.
Findings:
● Batch V20012607002 = manufactured 2026-07-08 to 2026-07-09 on ML-ST-01; subject
batch in deviation and internal audit packet.
● Batch V20012607003 = secondary packaging planning number created in ERP pre-
dispatch queue on 2026-07-10 for a later campaign; not yet manufactured.
● Distributor email referenced 07003 because the shipper packing list contained a
provisional outbound dispatch label generated from ERP staging, while vial-level and
pallet-level documents tie retained units to 07002.
Remaining uncertainty:
● One shipper-case manifest screenshot still displays 07003 in header while carton labels
inside are not visible in the evidence attached.
● No physical returned shipper has yet been received to conclusively reconcile outer
shipping identifiers against vial labels.
Document 2: Missing batch record pages recovered
Document ID: VISK-BMR-FPP2001-2607-002-ADD1
Version: R0
Owner: M. Deepak
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-UTL-002

Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0304-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-
ST-0309-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0303, EMP-0202, EMP-0417, EMP-0418
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Recovered pages: Page 19, 20, 21, and 26.
Page 19 summary:
● Intervention note: “Cap path adjusted – 14:10 after irregular skirt on sample vial.”
● Reason code field now visible and marked “minor mechanical adjustment,” but initials
appear overwritten.
Page 20 summary:
● Visual segregation note lists “3 units removed to tray ST1-HLD-27.”
● A second annotation in different handwriting says “2 units from same lane, 1 from post-
restart check.”
Page 21 summary:
● Quarantine transfer entry: “1 pallet + 8 shippers to QA hold at 17:35.”
● Warehouse signature present; QA hold receiver initials illegible.
Page 26 summary:
● Provisional disposition section marked “Pending QA assessment.”
● No final release signature.
● Electronic footer timestamp indicates page printed 2026-07-10 07:56, suggesting packet
assembly after manufacturing completion.
Notable discrepancy:
The earlier handwritten note implying release on 09 July is not supported by recovered Page
26, which shows pending QA assessment rather than release.
Document 3: Batch record audit-trail excerpt

Document ID: VISK-IT-RPT-2026-AT-0044
Version: R0
Owner: P. Singh
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0119-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0120-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0303, EMP-0202, EMP-0101, EMP-0801
Related Supplier: None
System events excerpt:
● 2026-07-09 14:12 – intervention comment field opened by user mdeepak.
● 2026-07-09 14:16 – value entered: “cap path adjusted.”
● 2026-07-09 17:42 – warehouse hold transfer field created by user nsk_packhold.
● 2026-07-10 07:51 – page 26 disposition section opened by user qlakshmi.
● 2026-07-10 07:53 – disposition field changed from blank to “pending QA assessment.”
● 2026-07-10 08:02 – print command executed for full packet copy.
Implication:
The audit trail supports post-run packet assembly and confirms that final disposition was not
completed in the system before the packet copy was printed.[web:76][web:77]
Remaining gap:
No system record yet explains the handwritten “release already completed 09/07 by QA?”
note seen on the original deviation copy.
Document 4: Complaint photo recovery note and
transcribed image metadata
Document ID: VISK-QA-RPT-2026-PH-0013
Version: R0
Owner: N. Lakshmi
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: Not directly visible
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0304-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0102-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002 (tentative)

Related Employees: EMP-0202, EMP-0101
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Recovered from distributor email archive: two compressed JPEG files and one mobile
screenshot.
Photo metadata transcription:
● Image 1 filename: IMG_4478.jpg; timestamp 2026-07-10 06:24 GST.
● Image 2 filename: IMG_4479.jpg; timestamp 2026-07-10 06:25 GST.
● Screenshot filename: shipper_label.jpg; timestamp unavailable.
Visual description summary:
● Image 1: one vial with cap visible at slight angle; faint off-white ring around stopper edge;
flip-off cap appears slightly lifted on one side.
● Image 2: three vials in carton cavity; one cap reflection makes comparison difficult; batch
text blurred.
● Screenshot: outer shipper label includes dispatch staging code ending in 07003, but
product carton image is not included.
Limitations:
● Metadata confirms timing but not batch identity.
● Compression artifacts reduce reliability of visual interpretation.
● No chain of custody from distributor to Aetheris yet established.
Document 5: Dispatch and quarantine chronology
Document ID: VISK-WH-RPT-2026-0016-R0
Version: R0
Owner: S. Naidu
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: ML-PKG-02; EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-WH-SOP-WH-0407-R0; PASH-WH-SOP-WH-0406-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002; dispatch staging reference 07003
Related Employees: EMP-0601, EMP-0202, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: SUP-008

Chronology:
● 2026-07-09 17:35 – pallet FP-07 and 8 shippers transferred to QA hold.
● 2026-07-10 05:58 – one export shipper already in outbound staging lane, linked in ERP to
provisional dispatch reference ending 07003.
● 2026-07-10 08:49 – dispatch freeze email received from QA.
● 2026-07-10 09:03 – staging lane physically blocked; no further loading performed.
● 2026-07-10 11:20 – warehouse recount found 7 shippers in hold plus 1 in staging
quarantine bay.
Discrepancy resolved partly:
There was no confirmed external shipment after the freeze, but one shipper appears to have
reached pre-dispatch staging before QA stop instruction.
Remaining uncertainty:
Commercial team has not confirmed whether the distributor complaint refers to previously
shipped stock from another lot or to advance photos shared by local warehouse staff.
Document 6: Equipment status history log
Document ID: VISK-QA-LOG-2026-ES-0081
Version: R0
Owner: T. Srinivas
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0204, EMP-0303, EMP-0401
Related Supplier: None
Status sequence:
● 2026-07-09 07:10 – Cleaned / Ready for Use.
● 2026-07-09 17:12 – Under Observation (temporary handwritten tag applied by production
supervisor).
● 2026-07-10 06:45 – Cleaned / Ready for Use form generated in logbook, apparently based
on standard post-cleaning routine.
● 2026-07-10 07:02 – Under Observation re-applied after QA walkthrough discussion.

Interpretation:
The contradictory status labels likely resulted from parallel use of handwritten temporary
tagging and formal status-form entry, rather than one stable status history.
Issue still open:
The person who generated the 06:45 “Ready for Use” entry has not provided a clarifying
statement yet.
Document 7: Calibration raw data page recovered
Document ID: VISK-CAL-2026-0018-RAW-P3
Version: R0
Owner: J. Jose
Effective Date: 2026-06-28
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-ENG-SOP-ENG-0607-R0; internal calibration worksheet reference
CWS-ST-14
Related Product: FPP-2001; FPP-2003
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0401, EMP-0103
Related Supplier: None
Recovered raw values:
● Crimp-force setpoint challenge: 11.2 / 11.1 / 11.3 N equivalent reference values.
● Acceptance band: 10.8 to 11.6.
● As-left result: pass.
● Comment: “tested with standard setup kit ST-CAP-A.”
New discrepancy introduced:
The current batch setup sheet references change parts ST-CAP-B, not ST-CAP-A.
Implication:
Calibration was acceptable, but the raw page does not confirm that the same setup-part
configuration used in the July batch was directly covered.

Sheet details:
● Change-part set installed: ST-CAP-B.
● Verified by: signature resembling R. Chandr....
● Engineering second-check field: blank.
● Trial crimp samples: 5 recorded.
● Result field: “acceptable visual profile.”
● Measurement field for crimp diameter: blank.
Concern:
The setup-verification sheet exists, but it is incomplete and lacks documented second-check
evidence.
Document 9: Contract-staff identity clarification
Document ID: CORP-HR-RPT-2026-0011-R0

● EMP-0417 R. Chandra = contract sterile operator onboarded 2026-06-24 through approved
staffing annex, not yet added to Phase 0.1 master employee list.
● EMP-0418 S. Fernandes = contract inspection aide assigned temporary support 2026-07-
08 to 2026-07-12.
Problem:
● Contract-staff annex exists but master employee register in the organization pack was not
updated.
● Temporary role of EMP-0418 is documented as “visual support,” but not clearly
designated as quality-critical inspection role.
Document 10: Contract-staff training proof
Document ID: VISK-TRN-2026-0027-ADD1
Version: R0
Owner: G. Patel
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0301-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-
ST-0309-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0417, EMP-0418, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: None
Recovered training data:
● EMP-0417 R. Chandra completed gowning qualification and supervised line operation
familiarization on 2026-06-29.
● EMP-0418 S. Fernandes completed GMP induction and gowning on 2026-07-07.
● No documented qualification found for independent cap/defect visual inspection decisions.
● Training assignment for “intervention documentation refresher” still open for both contract
staff.
Implication:
This resolves identity but not adequacy of qualification for the role actually performed during
the event.

Document 11: Supplier component lot and attachment
recovery
Document ID: SUPC-SUP-003-2026-002-ADD1
Version: R0
Owner: S. Naidu
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0301-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0601, EMP-0101
Related Supplier: SUP-003
Recovered supplier details:
● Vial lot: SG-V-44721.
● Stopper lot: STP-77419.
● Flip-off cap lot: CAP-18802.
● Supplier attachment 5 title: “Siliconization Process Summary for Stopper Component.”
Attachment summary:
The stopper siliconization summary indicates silicone level is controlled within supplier
internal specification. No out-of-trend flag is stated in the excerpt.
Limitation:
Only summary pages were recovered; full raw supplier process data not available in Aetheris
file.
Document 12: Packaging component traceability excerpt
Document ID: SUPC-SUP-008-2026-002

Traceability details:
● Carton artwork lot: PW-IC-7714.
● Shipper label stock lot: PW-SL-2288.
● Variable-data staging template revision: 04.
● ERP dispatch staging label profile maps product family FPP-2001 to next available
outbound queue unless QA hold flag is active.
Possible relevance:
This may explain why a provisional shipper screenshot displayed 07003 even if the physical
product remained 07002 in hold.
Not resolved:

Document 13: Laboratory follow-up request and
chemistry screen
Document ID: VISK-QC-RPT-2026-0276-ADD1
Version: R1
Owner: T. Srinivas
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-QC-003; outsourced FTIR screening note referenced without
attachment
Related SOP: VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0509-R0; VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0510-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0204, EMP-0102
Related Supplier: SUP-003
Follow-up note:
● Microbiology incubation ongoing; no conclusion.
● Preliminary chemistry swab screen described as “trace silicone-like response possible,”
but outsourced FTIR attachment pending.
● Sample quantity limited; repeat swab not possible on same vial.

Critical gap:
No final analytical identification is yet available, and the phrase “silicone-like response
possible” is not a conclusive lab determination.
Document 14: EM trend assessment memo
Document ID: VISK-QC-RPT-2026-0281-R0
Version: R0
Owner: T. Srinivas
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-UTL-002; EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0510-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0302-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0204, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: None
Assessment summary:
● Grade A and Grade B data for fill date 2026-07-08 to 2026-07-09 remain within
established limits.
● Grade C prep-room alert on 2026-07-08 is the third alert-level event in six weeks for that
room.
● No direct evidence currently links the Grade C alert to vial stopper-edge residue or cap
profile variation.
● One operator glove-print entry remained blank because second operator changed role after
restart and personnel monitoring sheet was not updated contemporaneously.
Importance:
This memo partially fills the missing EM trend summary noted in the audit, but it also
introduces a recurring low-grade environmental signal that may warrant broader
review.[web:64][web:86]
Document 15: Clarifying witness addenda

Document ID: VISK-QA-FRM-2026-WS-0006-ADD1
Version: R0
Owner: Meera Iyer
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0108-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0303, EMP-0401, EMP-0417, EMP-0418
Related Supplier: None
Addendum from M. Deepak:
“Three suspect units sounds more accurate than one. At least one was identified before guide
adjustment and one after restart, but I cannot confirm whether the third came from the same
lane.”
Addendum from J. Jose:
“The two empty-vial contact marks were from a setup challenge, but I cannot rule out that
similar contact could affect filled units if alignment drifted during operation.”
Addendum from R. Chandra:
“I signed the setup check but believed engineering would complete the second verification
field.”
Addendum from S. Fernandes:
“I helped separate unusual-looking units only under instruction and did not make
accept/reject decisions alone.”
Impact:
The addenda narrow some contradictions but still do not establish whether the issue was
mechanical, component-related, handling-related, cosmetic-only, or product-related.
Document 16: QA intake discrepancy tracker
Document ID: VISK-QA-LOG-2026-DT-0003
Version: R0
Owner: S. Balaji
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002; EQ-UTL-002
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0118-R0

Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002; reference conflict V20012607003
Related Employees: EMP-0201, EMP-0101, EMP-0202, EMP-0303, EMP-0204, EMP-0401
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Open discrepancies list:
1. Final shipped batch identity not fully confirmed from returned market sample.
2. Root significance of provisional ERP dispatch label remains operationally explained but
not physically verified.
3. Equipment-status control was inconsistent between handwritten and formal label systems.
4. Change-part setup verification incomplete; second-check absent.
5. Qualification adequacy of temporary staff for visual support remains unclear.
6. Lab data remains preliminary and inconclusive.
7. Relevance of recurring Grade C prep-room alerts undetermined.
8. Supplier siliconization summary is incomplete and not independently verified.
9. No final assessment yet on distributed product impact, if any.
Document 17: Email follow-up chain
Document ID: CORP-IT-RPT-2026-MSG-089
Version: R0
Owner: P. Singh
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002; ML-PKG-02
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0304-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002; V20012607003
Related Employees: EMP-0101, EMP-0202, EMP-0601, EMP-0501, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
2026-07-11 09:12 – From S. Naidu to QA and RA
“Staging screenshot confusion likely due to outbound queue template. Physical hold
inventory corresponds to 07002, but commercial partner may have seen export staging
reference.”
2026-07-11 09:34 – From H. Kaur
“Please confirm whether any cartons with 07002 were ever transferred to export consolidator.
If yes, complaint source may still be real and not just a screenshot mix-up.”

2026-07-11 10:07 – From Meera Iyer
“Treat complaint as potentially valid until physical reconciliation complete. No assumptions
that this is documentation-only.”
2026-07-11 10:41 – From M. Deepak
“Held tray and pallet remain under QA seal. No additional similar issue seen on retained units
beyond the originally segregated samples.”
Unresolved tension:
The emails reduce some confusion but preserve uncertainty about whether the market
complaint is tied to the same physical stock now on hold.
How this pack helps finalize model behavior
This follow-up evidence set allows a system to test whether it can request and incorporate
missing evidence without collapsing ambiguity too early. Strong QMS and GMP
documentation practice requires traceability across source records, raw evidence, audit trails,
qualification records, batch disposition history, and linked supplier or equipment information,
especially where inconsistencies appear in an audit or deviation
package.[web:65][web:76][web:86]
A robust investigation model should now be able to:
● Reconcile that 07002 is the likely manufacturing batch while 07003 may be a dispatch-
staging reference.
● Detect that product disposition remained pending rather than released at the time key
documents were printed.[web:76][web:77]
● Identify that temporary staff identity is now clarified but qualification adequacy remains
unresolved.
● Link mechanical adjustment evidence to incomplete setup verification and configuration-
specific calibration uncertainty.
● Recognize that environmental and laboratory data remain suggestive but not conclusive.
● Flag that supplier and packaging records explain some documentation confusion but not
the physical symptom itself.
Remaining intentionally unresolved points

The package is now more complete, but it still intentionally stops short of a solved
investigation so the model must reason across competing hypotheses:[web:65][web:77]
● Mechanical alignment or change-part issue.
● Cosmetic-only cap/skirt variance with no product-quality impact.
● Stopper/silicone/component contribution from supplier materials.
● Handling or packaging-induced artifact.
● Documentation/control failure that exaggerates or obscures the real technical issue.
● Broader process-control weakness involving setup verification, visual segregation, and
status labeling.
Current status
Event state: Open, evidence significantly expanded, still under investigation.
Root cause: Not established.
CAPA: Not initiated in this follow-up pack.
Use case: Missing-evidence response pack for Helix discrepancy-resolution workflows.
